summary: SPIKE: Fuzz test BSON libraries using GenAI
description:
{panel:borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#21313c|titleBGColor=#21313c|titleColor=#fafbfc|bgColor=#fafbfc}
h3. *Summary*
To improve our security posture and confidence, we want to fuzz test our BSON libraries across all supported languages. We propose a 2-week spike using [Devin|https://www.cognition.ai/blog/introducing-devin] from Cognition to quickly generate fuzz tests for one BSON library. We will evaluate the effort required and quality of the resulting tests to determine if this is a tenable path forward to increase our security test coverage for our drivers and related libraries.

h3. *Motivation*
h4. Who is the affected end user?
Users of our drivers and libraries.
h4. How does this affect the end user?
Security vulnerabilities require fixes, CVEs, and upgrades by end users.
h4. How likely is it that this problem or use case will occur?
All software can contain security vulnerabilities. The intention with this project is to detect and catch them early preferably before we ship a release.  
h4. If the problem does occur, what are the consequences and how severe are they?
The severity is highly dependent on the ease of exploit and consequences. Detecting vulnerabilities through fuzz testing will catch errors sooner and mitigate the reputational harm.
h4. Is this issue urgent?
We want to complete the spike in FY25Q4.
h4. Is this ticket required by a downstream team?
No.
h4. Is this ticket only for tests?
Yes.
h3. *Acceptance Criteria*
We will compare the ease of specifying requirements to Devin versus a human engineer and the quality of fuzz testing code produced. The desire is to reduce laborious, repetitive coding tasks and allow our human engineers to focus on higher-value work requiring human ingenuity. If this spike proves successful, we would plan to do Devin-implemented fuzz tests across other drivers in a future quarter. We also hope to learn whether further security-focused tests could be generated efficiently using Devin.


{panel}

summary: Drivers Support for indexing and querying on UUID and null datatype
description:
{panel:bgColor=#fafbfc}
h3. *Summary*

_Users can now natively query UUID and null fields within Atlas Search._ _This functionality will be supported by all MongoDB versions._ 
 * _[Index syntax for UUID|https://docs.google.com/document/d/1qLn6UIw4G63MX3ns8Gc5qPfgx2zN9JDratKMGygfSfg/edit#heading=h.ooj0sx4bj88q]_
 * [_Query syntax for UUID_|https://docs.google.com/document/d/1qLn6UIw4G63MX3ns8Gc5qPfgx2zN9JDratKMGygfSfg/edit#heading=h.ba1k3fenrqd3]
 * [_Query syntax for null_|https://docs.google.com/document/d/1qLn6UIw4G63MX3ns8Gc5qPfgx2zN9JDratKMGygfSfg/edit#heading=h.lqwqdat14tgg]

h3. *Motivation*
h4. Who is the affected end user?

_Backend, Fullstack developer_
h4. How does this affect the end user?

_Users can now easily index and query UUID and null fields._
h4. How likely is it that this problem or use case will occur?

_Main path_
h4. If the problem does occur, what are the consequences and how severe are they?

_Previously, users had to store, index and query their UUIDs and nulls as 'string' fields to use with Atlas Search. This results in data duplication, results in poor code hygiene, and is not acceptable for users who use strictly typed languages._ 
h4. Is this issue urgent?

_Does this ticket have a required timeline? What is it?_
h4. Is this ticket required by a downstream team?

_Needed by Atlas Search_
h4. Is this ticket only for tests?

_Functional improvement_
h3. *Acceptance Criteria*

_Users must be able to query for_
 * _UUID types using the equals and in operator_
 * _null using the equals operator_

_Additionally, users must be able to specify UUID fields for Atlas Search sorting._
{panel}

summary: Do not send buildInfo or buildinfo to check for feature compatibility
description:
{panel:bgColor=#fafbfc}
h3. *Summary*

Drivers must not use buildInfo to detect server feature compatibility because buildInfo is not included in the versioned API.
h3. *Motivation*

The [specification README|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/df6be82f865e9b72444488fd62ae1eb5fca18569/README.rst#check-wire-version-not-server-version] notes:
{quote}When determining server capabilities within the driver, rely only on the maxWireVersion in the hello response, not on the X.Y.Z server version.
{quote}
CSHARP-3480 describes a driver using the {{buildInfo}} command as part of the connection handshake. {{buildInfo}} is not part of the [versioned API|https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/versioned-api/#api-v1-commands] and will return an error if sent with apiStrict. Example:
{code}$ mongosh --apiVersion=1 --apiStrict --eval "db.runCommand({buildInfo:1})"
Current Mongosh Log ID: 61194b0fea88cad1d1192c7a
Connecting to:          mongodb://127.0.0.1:27017/?directConnection=true&serverSelectionTimeoutMS=2000
Using MongoDB:          (API Version 1)
Using Mongosh:          1.0.4

For mongosh info see: https://docs.mongodb.com/mongodb-shell/

MongoServerError: Provided apiStrict:true, but the command buildInfo is not in API Version 1. Information on supported commands and migrations in API Version 1 can be found at https://dochub.mongodb.org/core/manual-versioned-api
{code}
The server may remove full support for {{buildInfo}} in the future.
h4. Who is the affected end user?

All driver users.
h4. How does this affect the end user?

If buildinfo or buildInfo is removed from the server in the future, this could prevent a driver that is sending buildinfo or buildInfo from connecting to a server.
h4. How likely is it that this problem or use case will occur?

buildinfo / buildInfo is not included in the versioned API. This will prevent the server
h4. If the problem does occur, what are the consequences and how severe are they?

A problem would be high severity. If buildInfo is removed from the server, drivers that are sending buildInfo as part of connection establishment will fail to connect.
h4. Is this issue urgent?

TBD. This will be a requirement when the server removes buildInfo.
h4. Is this ticket required by a downstream team?

It may be necessary to coordinate with the server team. A driver that has not resolved this will not be able to connect to an upcoming server release that removes buildInfo.
h4. Is this ticket only for tests?

No. There is functional impact.
h3. *Scope*

Add a note to the Handshake specification to prohibit any other commands being sent to determine feature compatibility.

Add a new specification test in the unified test format to test this behavior.
- Enable a failpoint on the buildInfo and buildinfo commands to return a network error.
- Send a command (could be ping).
- Expect success. Connection establishment should fail on drivers sending buildInfo/buildinfo.
{panel}

summary: OIDC teardown script fails with "ERROR: CLUSTER_NAME not set"
description:
{panel:borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#21313c|titleBGColor=#21313c|titleColor=#fafbfc|bgColor=#fafbfc}
h3. *Summary*

The teardown phase of the [OIDC auth tests|https://github.com/mongodb-labs/drivers-evergreen-tools/tree/master/.evergreen/auth_oidc] fails with this error:

{noformat}
ERROR: CLUSTER_NAME not set
{noformat}

I believe it's coming from [teardown-atlas-cluster.sh|https://github.com/mongodb-labs/drivers-evergreen-tools/blob/4a84579f625b92f982bce1a058518a23962935cf/.evergreen/atlas/teardown-atlas-cluster.sh#L30], which is called by [auth_oidc/teardown.sh|https://github.com/mongodb-labs/drivers-evergreen-tools/blob/4a84579f625b92f982bce1a058518a23962935cf/.evergreen/auth_oidc/teardown.sh#L16]. The root cause seems to be that {{CLUSTER_NAME}} is not set. The [Azure|https://github.com/mongodb-labs/drivers-evergreen-tools/tree/master/.evergreen/auth_oidc/azure] and [GCP|https://github.com/mongodb-labs/drivers-evergreen-tools/tree/master/.evergreen/auth_oidc/gcp] OIDC tests don't have the same problem, but they write {{CLUSTER_NAME}} to file in a different path (see [auth_oidc/gcp/setup.sh|https://github.com/mongodb-labs/drivers-evergreen-tools/blob/4a84579f625b92f982bce1a058518a23962935cf/.evergreen/auth_oidc/gcp/setup.sh#L22] vs [auth_oidc/setup.sh|https://github.com/mongodb-labs/drivers-evergreen-tools/blob/4a84579f625b92f982bce1a058518a23962935cf/.evergreen/auth_oidc/setup.sh#L41]).

The result is that the teardown phase fails consistently, possibly leaving Atlas resources running.

Confirmed this impacts at least the [Rust Driver|https://evergreen.mongodb.com/task_log_raw/mongo_rust_driver_oidc_linux_oidc_auth_test_latest_7429896ea2f15e1046b9aabb73c4f00f83af20f3_25_03_20_20_19_42/0?type=T#L1505] and [Go Driver|https://spruce.mongodb.com/task/mongo_go_driver_testoidc_variant_oidc_auth_test_a4c0170403e5f6a01ee191dc1e84b3becc7012d2_25_03_21_19_50_47/logs?execution=0]

h3. *Motivation*
h4. Who is the affected end user?

Drivers devs, possibly DBX leadership (if we're spending money on unterminated Atlas resources).

h4. How does this affect the end user?

OIDC Evergreen tasks may fail. Maybe wastes money.

h4. How likely is it that this problem or use case will occur?

It will always occur when using the standard OIDC test setup and teardown scripts.

h4. If the problem does occur, what are the consequences and how severe are they?

Confusion about the failed OIDC Evergreen task, possibly blocked driver development and/or releases. Possible wasted money.

h4. Is this issue urgent?
No.

h4. Is this ticket required by a downstream team?
No.

h4. Is this ticket only for tests?
Yes.
h3. *Acceptance Criteria*
Update OIDC test teardown scripts to correctly tear down resources.

{panel}

summary: Include topologyClosedEvent and topologyOpeningEvent in unified test format
Unable to run AWS KMS test in Evergreen due to insufficient permissions for drivers-test-secrets-role
Fix support for MongoDB 4.0 in mongo-orchestration
description:
{panel:bgColor=#fafbfc}
h3. *Summary*

mongo-orchestration fails when trying to start MongoDB 4.0 deployments. The root cause seems to be that the latest version of pymongo dropped support for MongoDB 4.0.

E.g. [error log|https://spruce.mongodb.com/task/mongo_go_driver_tests_rhel_40_with_snappy_support__version~4.0_os_ssl_40~rhel87_64_test_replicaset_auth_nossl_910eb268789a1e9821525b5ec233f90c694fb049_25_08_06_19_08_34/logs?execution=0]:
{noformat}
   File "drivers-evergreen-tools/.local/uv-tool/drivers-orchestration/lib/python3.13/site-packages/mongo_orchestration/common.py", line 130, in connected
     client.admin.command('isMaster')
     ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^^^^^^
   ...
   File "drivers-evergreen-tools/.local/uv-tool/drivers-orchestration/lib/python3.13/site-packages/pymongo/topology_description.py", line 168, in check_compatible
     raise ConfigurationError(self._incompatible_err)
 pymongo.errors.ConfigurationError: Server at localhost:27017 reports wire version 7, but this version of PyMongo requires at least 8 (MongoDB 4.2).
{noformat}

h3. *Motivation*
h4. Who is the affected end user?
Drivers devs who still need to test on MongoDB 4.0

h4. How does this affect the end user?
The team's Evergreen CI scripts break.

h4. How likely is it that this problem or use case will occur?
All MongoDB 4.0 tasks fail.

h4. If the problem does occur, what are the consequences and how severe are they?
Teams are unable to run their Evergreen CI tasks for MongoDB 4.0

h4. Is this issue urgent?
It may block teams from releasing if they depend on passing tests for supported MongoDB versions.

h4. Is this ticket required by a downstream team?
No.

h4. Is this ticket only for tests?
Yes.

h3. *Acceptance Criteria*
* Update mongo-orchestration to use a version of pymongo that supports MongoDB 4.0
** See a previous [PR|https://github.com/mongodb-labs/drivers-evergreen-tools/pull/585] that fixed support for MongoDB 3.6 in mongo-orchestration.
* Should we pin the pymongo version to 4.11 so we don't have to repeat this fix every time pymongo drops support for a MongoDB version?

{panel}

summary: Drivers evergreen tools run-orchestration.sh does not download x64 binaries to arm Macs
description:
{panel:bgColor=#fafbfc}
h3. *Summary*

The run-orchestration.sh script seems to assume (understandably) the architecture I'm on is the only one I would like downloaded. Older server versions that were never built on arm macs can still be run and tested we just need to adjust the download script to obtain them when arm does not exist:

h3. *Motivation*
h4. Who is the affected end user?

A driver engineer on an ARM Mac trying to test an older server version

h4. How does this affect the end user?

Can't use the usual run-orchestration.sh entry point to our evg tools.

h4. How likely is it that this problem or use case will occur?

Local development main path

h4. If the problem does occur, what are the consequences and how severe are they?

{code}
INFO     Download archive 4.0-enterprise for osx-arm64
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "drivers-tools/.evergreen/orchestration/drivers-orchestration", line 10, in <module>
    sys.exit(main())
  File "drivers-tools/.evergreen/orchestration/drivers_orchestration.py", line 427, in main
    run(opts)
  File "drivers-tools/.evergreen/orchestration/drivers_orchestration.py", line 199, in run
    mongodl(shlex.split(args))
  File "drivers-tools/.local/uv-tool/drivers-orchestration/lib/python3.10/site-packages/mongodl.py", line 1161, in main
    result = _dl_component(
  File "drivers-tools/.local/uv-tool/drivers-orchestration/lib/python3.10/site-packages/mongodl.py", line 830, in _dl_component
    dl_url = _published_build_url(
  File "drivers-tools/.local/uv-tool/drivers-orchestration/lib/python3.10/site-packages/mongodl.py", line 761, in _published_build_url
    raise ValueError(
ValueError: No download was found for version="4.0" target="osx" arch="arm64" edition="enterprise" component="archive"
{code}

Script cannot complete launching MongoDB 

h4. Is this issue urgent?

No, there are alternatives, this is inconvenient.

h4. Is this ticket required by a downstream team?

No

h4. Is this ticket only for tests?

I assume no one is testing x64-only servers on ARM macs so not for tests.

h3. *Acceptance Criteria*
* -If an ARM binary cannot be found when on a darwin platform attempt to find and use an x64 binary.-
* Add an option to run-orchestration to supply the target architecture to allow downloading x86_64 binaries on arm64 macOS.

{panel}

summary: Mongo-orchestration fails to assign multiple unique ports the first time
description:
{panel:bgColor=#fafbfc}
h3. *Summary*

mongo-orchestration returns a 500 "internal server error" when asked to create multiple servers with unique ports (by leaving the port empty) the first time this is requested. Making the same request again will succeed, so this appears to be a race condition on the first time mongo-orchestration populates its list of available ports. I can reproduce this with the following request for a basic replica set with two nodes:

 
{code:java}
$ curl -XPOST --data "{\"id\": \"rs0\", \"members\": [{}, {}]}" "http://localhost:8889/v1/replica_sets"

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/ubuntu/.local/lib/python3.10/site-packages/mongo_orchestration/apps/__init__.py", line 66, in wrap
    return f(*arg, **kwd)
  File "/home/ubuntu/.local/lib/python3.10/site-packages/mongo_orchestration/apps/replica_sets.py", line 80, in rs_create
    result = _rs_create(data)
  File "/home/ubuntu/.local/lib/python3.10/site-packages/mongo_orchestration/apps/replica_sets.py", line 37, in _rs_create
    rs_id = ReplicaSets().create(params)
  File "/home/ubuntu/.local/lib/python3.10/site-packages/mongo_orchestration/replica_sets.py", line 658, in create
    repl = ReplicaSet(rs_params)
  File "/home/ubuntu/.local/lib/python3.10/site-packages/mongo_orchestration/replica_sets.py", line 95, in __init__
    if not self.repl_init(config):
  File "/home/ubuntu/.local/lib/python3.10/site-packages/mongo_orchestration/replica_sets.py", line 210, in repl_init
    result = self.connection(init_server).admin.command("replSetInitiate", config)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.10/dist-packages/pymongo/_csot.py", line 119, in csot_wrapper
    return func(self, *args, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.10/dist-packages/pymongo/synchronous/database.py", line 930, in command
    return self._command(
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.10/dist-packages/pymongo/synchronous/database.py", line 770, in _command
    return conn.command(
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.10/dist-packages/pymongo/synchronous/helpers.py", line 47, in inner
    return func(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.10/dist-packages/pymongo/synchronous/pool.py", line 536, in command
    return command(
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.10/dist-packages/pymongo/synchronous/network.py", line 213, in command
    helpers_shared._check_command_response(
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.10/dist-packages/pymongo/helpers_shared.py", line 247, in _check_command_response
    raise OperationFailure(errmsg, code, response, max_wire_version)
pymongo.errors.OperationFailure: Found two member configurations with same host field, members.0.host == members.1.host == localhost:1030, full error: {'ok': 0.0, 'errmsg': 'Found two member configurations with same host field, members.0.host == members.1.host == localhost:1030', 'code': 93, 'codeName': 'InvalidReplicaSetConfig'} {code}
 
h3. *Motivation*
h4. Who is the affected end user?

Developers may experience this the first time an integration test is run. See [https://jira.mongodb.org/browse/TUNE-197] 

We can work around this issue in our CI by requesting mongo-orchestration to create a single server (with no port assigned). This ensures that the port pool is initialized and subsequent requests succeed.
h4. How does this affect the end user?

Developers will be confused by this error and may spend time trying to track down what went wrong in a test when it is really mongo-orchestration at fault.
h4. How likely is it that this problem or use case will occur?

Every first run of an integration test on a new mongo-orchestration instance is affected.
h4. If the problem does occur, what are the consequences and how severe are they?

Annoyance/distraction/time-sink.
h4. Is this issue urgent?

It would be ideal to fix this ASAP, but the workaround of retrying the request makes this technically non-blocking.
h4. Is this ticket required by a downstream team?

Mongotune.
h4. Is this ticket only for tests?

Just tests / dev workflow.
h3. *Acceptance Criteria*

Basic request of mongo-orchestration assigning ports automatically should succeed on the first try.
{panel}

summary: Allow update to supply sort option
description:
{panel:title=Original Downstream Change Summary|titleBGColor=#239eb0|bgColor=#c2d2c2}
    Drivers should use the new syntax allow a sort option to be supplied to an update command.
    h2. Description of Linked Ticket
    Unlike findAndModify, updateOne does not accept sort so in cases on match on more than one candidate document there is no way to control which will be updated.

This is request to add such a option.

summary: $dateToString aggregation with timezone outputs invalid ISO8601 string
description:
{panel:title=Original Downstream Change Summary|titleBGColor=#239eb0|bgColor=#c2d2c2}
    The current default behavior of MongoDB's $dateToString aggregation expression is incorrect because it violates ISO 8601. (It tacks on a "Z" to the end of the string by default, which implies that it is in UTC [1], but in actually it's in the timezone of the local server.)

This ticket proposes a number of routes to change the default behavior of $dateToString to bring it into compliance with ISO 8601.

[1] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601#Coordinated_Universal_Time_(UTC)
    h2. Description of Linked Ticket
    When using the {{$dateToString}} aggregation operator on an ISODate value, specifying a timezone but not a format string, an invalid/inaccurate ISO8601 date string is returned. 

Specifically: {{2023-04-26T12:06:17.194Z}} when using something like:

{code:JavaScript}
db.collection.aggregate([{ "$addFields": {"txDateString": { "$dateToString": {date: "$transactionDateTS", timezone: "America/New_York"} } }} ])`
{code}

The root issue is that the "Z" *categorically implies* a timezone of UTC (+00:00)... But, in this case, that's absolutely incorrect and, technically, makes it an invalid ISO8601 date string. (I suppose one could argue that it's *syntatically* valid - but it's certainly misleading and could result in major issues if another program were to parse the date string as it would interpret the time as "Zulu"/UTC because of the "Z".) The inclusion of the "Z" when *explicitly specifying* a timezone is certainly unexpected...  

At a *minimum*, when a format string is unspecified and a date/time in UTC is converted to another time zone, the "Z" should be left off. Ideally, the UTC offset (obtained from the timezone conversion) should be included as per ISO8601 -- i.e. in this case, {{2023-04-26T12:06:17.194-04:00}} (at the time of this writing, {{America/New_York}} is on Eastern Daylight Time (EDT) which is UTC-04:00.) 

Related issues appear to include: SERVER-33173 and SERVER-28610

summary: mongo-orchestration racy failure when creating sharded clusters
description:
{panel:bgColor=#fafbfc}
h3. *Summary*

Mongo-orchestration sometimes fails to create a sharded cluster with a 500 error. This race seems to happen more frequently when auth is enabled, and when the number of shards increases.

  File "/home/ubuntu/.local/lib/python3.10/site-packages/pymongo/helpers.py", line 181, in _check_command_response
    raise OperationFailure(errmsg, code, response, max_wire_version)
pymongo.errors.OperationFailure: Another addShard with different arguments is already running with different options, full error: {'ok': 0.0, 'errmsg': 'Another addShard with different arguments is already running with different options', 'code': 117, 'codeName': 'ConflictingOperationInProgress', '$clusterTime': {'clusterTime': Timestamp(1771891141, 61), 'signature': {'hash': b'\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00', 'keyId': 0}}, 'operationTime': Timestamp(1771891141, 60)} {code}
An example command that can be used to trigger this race (if needed bump up the number of shards):
{code:java}
curl -XPOST --data "{\"id\": \"shard-test0\", \"auth_key\": \"secret\", \"login\": \"admin\", \"password\": \"password\", \"shards\": [{\"id\": \"sh00\", \"shardParams\": {\"members\": [{}, {}, {}]}}, {\"id\": \"sh01\", \"shardParams\": {\"members\": [{}, {}, {}]}}, {\"id\": \"sh02\", \"shardParams\": {\"members\": [{}, {}, {}]}}, {\"id\": \"sh03\", \"shardParams\": {\"members\": [{}, {}, {}]}}, {\"id\": \"sh04\", \"shardParams\": {\"members\": [{}, {}, {}]}}], \"routers\": [{}]}" "http://localhost:8889/v1/sharded_clusters" {code}
h3. *Motivation*
h4. Who is the affected end user?

_We use mongo-orchestration in our integration testing._
h4. How does this affect the end user?

_This causes sporadic failures on our CI._
h4. How likely is it that this problem or use case will occur?

_The race seems to occur about 1/20 runs on our CI._
h4. If the problem does occur, what are the consequences and how severe are they?

_Test failures._
h4. Is this issue urgent?

_No timeline, we have a retry workaround in place, but it is not ideal._
h4. Is this ticket required by a downstream team?

_TUNE._
h4. Is this ticket only for tests?

_Testing only._
h3. *Acceptance Criteria*

_Fix the bug._
{panel}

summary: Add spec test for server deprioritization with changed ServerDescription
description:
h3. *Summary*
The server selection spec states that deprioritization should use "only server addresses and not ServerDescription objects" to ensure a deprioritized server cannot become eligible for retry due to topology changes. However, the existing spec tests all use identical ServerDescriptions between topology_description.servers and deprioritized_servers, so drivers using full ServerDescription equality instead of address-only comparison will incorrectly pass all tests. 

h3. *Motivation*

Avoid introducing buggy code that attempts to filter candidates based on a server object, e.g. {{\{27017, RSPrimary, 50ms\} != \{27017, RSSecondary, 5ms\}}} would incorrectly pass the filter, allowing the same server (with changed state) to be selected for retry.

h3. *Acceptance Criteria*
Add a unified spec test to cover the following case: 

# Client sends operation to b:27017 (RSPrimary, RTT 50ms)
# Operation fails 
# Driver adds b:27017 to deprioritized list with its current state
# Topology monitor updates and b:27017 stepped down (RSSecondary, RTT 5ms)
# Ensure that b:2717 does not pass the filter and does not get selected for retry

summary: Auth-ssl configuration does not enforce auth on standalone with mongodb-runner
description:
{panel:borderStyle=solid|borderColor=#21313c|titleBGColor=#21313c|titleColor=#fafbfc|bgColor=#fafbfc}
h3. *Summary*
When starting a standalone with auth and ssl using {{run-mongodb.sh}} authentication is not enforced. This may cause failures for tests expecting authentication error.

This bug was hidden because {{run-orchestration.sh}} uses the mongo-orchestration HTTP API, which internally added --auth to mongod whenever login/password were present in the config, regardless of auth_key. run-mongodb.sh bypasses mongo-orchestration and passes the config directly to mongodb-runner, which does not have this implicit behavior and relies solely on what is in the config file. 

h3. *Motivation*
h4. Who is the affected end user?
Drivers developers
h4. How does this affect the end user?
Not affected
h4. How likely is it that this problem or use case will occur?
If a driver has tests that expect authentication enforced on a standalone
h4. If the problem does occur, what are the consequences and how severe are they?
Test failures
h4. Is this issue urgent?
No
h4. Is this ticket required by a downstream team?
No
h4. Is this ticket only for tests?
Yes
h3. *Acceptance Criteria*
Bug is fixed and the fix is tested.


{panel}

summary: drivers should use $query and $orderby in query spec instead of query and orderby
description:
1) dbclient.cpp
2) shell

summary: Commands sent to mongos sometimes break when sending $readPreference
description:
This ticket is meant to work around the issues described in SERVER-2474, SERVER-7768, SERVER-7789, etc.

As of the MongoDB 2.2-era driver releases, drivers send a $readPreference subdocument with some commands to mongos. Pre-2.4 versions of mongos throw an error when they receive this unexpected field.

Drivers should follow this algorithm:

If read preference is PRIMARY, don't send $readPreference to mongos. The default is PRIMARY so sending $readPreference is redundant anyway. If the ReadPreference is SecondaryPreferred *and no tags have been specified* do not send $readPreference. If read preference is anything other than PRIMARY always set the slaveOk bit.

summary: Drivers should check the query failure bit even on getmore response
description:
The query failure bit (bit 1 in the responseFlags field of the server's reply message - see link below) should be checked for replies to OP_GET_MORE.  See SERVER-9738 for a related server issue.

http://docs.mongodb.org/meta-driver/latest/legacy/mongodb-wire-protocol/#op-reply

summary: Drivers should only talk to servers with overlapping wire versions
description:
Drivers must assume a value of 0 for both minWireVersion and maxWireVersion if those fields are missing from the isMaster response, in order to preserve compatibility with servers prior to 2.5.2.

Additionally, each version of a driver must have its own min and max wire version, representing the range of servers that it is compatible with.

summary: JSON parsing bug for quoted keys
description: This is fixed in mgo.v2 here: https://github.com/10gen/mgo/commit/1826d82b4485bcededc6841c7bdae2de3a240b9b

summary: SlaveOk bit not set when connected directly to a secondary
description: When connected directly (connect=direct), the slaveOk bit isn't set when connected to a secondary.

summary: Use connect timeout as socket timeout for heartbeat connections
description: Relevant section: https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/server-discovery-and-monitoring/server-discovery-and-monitoring.rst#socket-timeout-for-monitoring-is-connecttimeoutms

summary: Handle socket timeout's differently
description: Currently, socket timeout's are controlled by using the current context's time remaining (if any). This means we are tying a user's request directly to whether we kill a socket. We should come up with a different strategy so that we don't tear down sockets artificially and throw away the potentially expensive setup we've already done.

summary: When a read/write error occurs, the connection pool should be cleared.
description: Currently, when a read/write error occurs, we request an immediate heartbeat. This is incorrect. Rather, we should be clearing the connection pool.

summary: Need to support server Tags
description:
2 Problems currently with tags:

1. https://github.com/10gen/mongo-go-driver/blob/master/internal/results.go#L33

This should be a bson.D, not a []bson.D

2. https://github.com/10gen/mongo-go-driver/blob/master/model/server.go#L66

Just needs to actually be done

summary: Fix hostname resolution during server discover
description: Currently, server discovery doesn't work for hosts that have a capital letter in them. This is because of an issue with how updates are applied to the cluster's FSM.

summary: Panic from cluster monitor closing channel during Stop
description:
When i start it with {{./bin/mongosqld -vvv}}, I get this panic after sampling is completed:
{noformat}panic: send on closed channel

goroutine 36 [running]:
github.com/10gen/sqlproxy/vendor/github.com/10gen/mongo-go-driver/cluster.(*Monitor).startMonitoringServer.func1(0xc420138240, 0xc420138120)
    /Users/wisdom/gopath/src/github.com/10gen/sqlproxy/vendor/github.com/10gen/mongo-go-driver/cluster/monitor.go:188 +0x92
created by github.com/10gen/sqlproxy/vendor/github.com/10gen/mongo-go-driver/cluster.(*Monitor).startMonitoringServer
    /Users/wisdom/gopath/src/github.com/10gen/sqlproxy/vendor/github.com/10gen/mongo-go-driver/cluster/monitor.go:190 +0x12f```
{noformat}
The panic is caused by this line: https://github.com/10gen/sqlproxy/blob/1ed515047a089fed7c64906948622743252eafeb/server/server.go#L103 though it's not clear why the driver bugs out because of that {{Close()}}.

I suspect it is a problem in the Go driver but haven't been able to pin it down.

summary: isMaster during heartbeat is retried even when successful
description: When performing an isMaster command during a heartbeat, the monitor currently retries even if the first isMaster is successful.

summary: Don't execute buildInfo command on every heartbeat
description: There's no need to execute buildInfo on every heartbeat, since the server version can't change without the socket being closed.  Not a big deal perf-wise but it does spam the mongodb server logs.

summary: Data race tests fail
description: Go currently reports race conditions in our server tests. I've attached the output of `make test-race` to the ticket.

summary: Variables in evergreen yml not correctly passed to mongo-orchestration
description:
The evergreen function used to start mongo-orchesteration:
{noformat}
  bootstrap-mongo-orchestration:                                                
    - command: shell.exec                                                       
      params:                                                                   
        script: |                                                               
          ${PREPARE_SHELL}                                                      
          MONGODB_VERSION=${VERSION} TOPOLOGY=${TOPOLOGY} AUTH=${AUTH} SSL=${SSL} sh ${DRIVERS_TOOLS}/.evergreen/run-orchestration.sh
    - command: expansions.update                                                
      params:                                                                   
        file: mo-expansion.yml 
{noformat}
This function requires variables VERSION,TOPOLOGY,AUTH, etc. to determine how to start the cluster.

Currently, we don't pass args to it, only to the run-tests function, and as a result are not correctly starting up the cluster we think were are.
Ex:
{noformat}
    - name: test-replicaset-auth-nossl                                          
      tags: ["test", "replicaset"]                                              
      commands:                                                                 
        - func: bootstrap-mongo-orchestration                                   
        - func: run-tests                                                       
          vars:                                                                 
            TOPOLOGY: "replica_set"                                             
            AUTH: "auth"                                                        
            SSL: "nossl"    
{noformat}

summary: Create separate package for Decimal128 type
description: The Decimal128 type is used in various places. To avoid placing it in a random package that isn't imported by anything, create a dedicated package to hold the Decimal128 type. Consolidate the two version of Decimal128 (bson.Decimal128 and ast.Decimal128) into this single type and update all references.

summary: Return 2 uint32's for Timestamp for Element/ReaderElement
description: The Element and ReaderElement types return a uint64 for the timestamp. Return 2 uint32's for the timestamp instead.

summary: Implement the Concat method for bson.Document
description: Implement the Combine method on the bson.Document type. This method will allow appending the elements of another BSON document to the current bson.Document. The combine method should support appending from another Document, from a []byte, and from an io.Reader. Unless there is a pressing reason to not support it, this method could be variadic to support combining multiple documents.

summary: Add function to create bson.Reader from an io.Reader
description: Add a function that will take an io.Reader as a parameter and return a bson.Reader.

summary: Update NewDocument to take a variadic list of *Elements
description:
Currently the NewDocument function takes a uint which is used as the initial capacity for the slices of the *Document. This was done primarily to enable users to allocate a single array upfront and then append to it later.

Instead of doing it that way, update NewDocument to take a variadic list of *Elements. If a user wishes to control allocations, they can first make a []*Element, fill it with documents, and then pass that to NewDocument. In NewDocument we'll reuse the provided slice to ensure we don't allocate an extra array.

For the majority of use cases, this change will enable a more concise creation of documents.

summary: BSON Library Decoder Panics And Cannot Cast Numbers
description:
The two main bugs:
* The decoder can't properly decode an int32 into a struct field that's an int64. As long as the values fit, we should be able to freely put int32's, int64's, and doubles into the other number types.
* The decoder cannot handle decoding into a slice when it is a field of a struct. I didn't check for maps, but from the code it looks like whenever a BSON Array is returned and we try to decode it into an array or a slice we panic.

Additionally, the unit test coverage is sparse. One idea to boost the unit test coverage would be to ensure that we support roundtripping: If a user gives us a struct to encode, we should be able to encode it and then decode back into the same type and the values should be the same. Writing test that cover the happy path for the getReflectValue method would also boost coverage.

For the slice case, it is okay to create a new slice of the correct length and assign it to the field, if users want to control their allocs they can handle unmarshaling in custom code.

summary: Eliminate internal use of panic() wherever possible
description:
There are a number of internal functions that panic when hitting an unexpected situation, e.g. {{message_query.AddMeta}}.  

Many of these are effectively "should never get here" sentinels, but nonetheless, these should be converted to return errors whenever possible.  When not possible, we *must* ensure such panics are caught with deferred recover() calls.

summary: SDAM spec tests failing on Windows
description: After the new BSON library get merged into the driver, the SDAM spec tests are failing on Windows due to not being able to unmarshal the extended JSON ObjectId's in the JSON file. We should figure out why this is happening and fix it.

summary: Check for $out in aggregation
description: Currently,  we use the primary read preference for all aggregations due to the complexity of checking an `interface{}` for "$out". Once we have an insertion-ordered map type implemented, we should check the pipeline for "$out" and select a server and read preference accordingly.

summary: Return error if the any lengths are negative
description: Since lengths in BSON are int32's we could have invalid BSON where this value is negative. The code should catch this whenever we read an int32 and return an error if the value is negative instead of potentially panicking or causing some error further down the call chain.

summary: Rename bson.ArrayConstructor to bson.ValueConstructor
description: When we originally designed the bson.ArrayConstructor we did not have a value type and it was returning elements. Now that it is returns value types, which are useful outside of the context of an Array, we should rename it to ValueConstructor. We might also want to rename Constructor to ElementConstructor to provide parity.

summary: bson.Reader.Lookup should return ErrElementNotFound if no element is found
description: Currently, bson.Reader.Lookup returns (nil, nil) if no element is found. For consistency with bson.Document, it should return (nil, ErrElementNotFound) in this case.

summary: Improve Document.ElementAt usability
description: Currently, the Document type has 2 methods for looking up an element at an index: ElementAt and MustElementAt. Since they sort far apart and don't have the same prefix it is difficult for users to find. To enable a more discoverable API and to more closely align with the semantics of the slice type in Go, change ElementAt to just return the Element and panic if out of bounds, remove the MustElementAt method, and add an ElementAtOK method, which will return false if the index is out of bounds. This provides a safety option while also making the two methods more discoverable.

summary: Add DecodeBytes method to the Cursor
description: The BI Connector team would like bytes returned directly from a Cursor, so add the DecodeBytes method to the Cursor interface and implementation.

summary: Add FromHex function to the objectid package
description: There is currently a method to turn an ObjectID into a hex string, but there is no way to create an ObjectID from a hex string. Implement a FromHex function in the objectid package that will allow a user to provide a hex string and get an ObjectID back or an error if the string is not a valid ObjectID.

summary: Clarify the documentation for the bson package wrt the builder.Builder type
description:
The [documentation|https://godoc.org/github.com/mongodb/mongo-go-driver/bson] says:

> The Builder type is used to create a BSON document. The type only allows the iterative building of a document, so there is no way to verify the contents outside of writing the document. If you have a Builder and need to conditionally add a field, you can write the document to a byte slice and use the Reader type to lookup the desired document, but in this case you should probably use a Document instead.

Hoever there is no `Builder` type defined anywhere in that package, which is misleading.

summary: Make mongo.Cursor its own interface
description: Currently in GoDoc, the documentation for mongo.Cursor does not indicate the interface definition, instead it just references the ops.Cursor documentation. Since we generally don't want users looking at this documentation, we should redefine mongo.Cursor to be an interface matching ops.Cursor.

summary: Add a method to bson.Value to get the offset into the underlying []byte
description: The BI Connector team would like to be able to get the offset for an underlying value type so they can reuse the byte slice for strings. Add a method to the Value type to get the Offset for the value.
